enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«In many cities around the world, the rise of ride-sharing applications has fundamentally altered urban transportation networks. Proponen ts argue that these services reduce the need for private car ownership, lower greenhouse gas emissions by optimizing routes, and provide flexible employment opportunities. However,  critics point out that they often increase overall traffic congestion by adding more vehicles to the road and pull passengers away from public transit systems.»

According to th e text, what is one of the negative effects of ride-sharing applications?

opciones.A: They completely eliminate flexible employment opportunities.
opciones.B: They optimize routes and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
opciones.C: They draw commuters away from public transportation networks.
opciones.D: They discourage people from buying private cars.

justificacion: El texto menciona explícitamente al final que los críticos señalan que estos servicios 'pull passengers away from public transit systems' (alejan a los pasajeros de los sistemas d e transporte público). Las opciones B y D son argumentos positivos descritos por los defensores, y la opción A contradice el texto.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Although honeybees are often credited with being the most vital pollinators for agriculture, recent ecological studies highlight the uns ung role of nocturnal insects, such as moths. While bees dominate daytime pollination, moths quietly manage a vast network of wildflowers and crops under the cover of darkness. Unf ortunately, expanding light pollution in suburban areas severely disrupts their navigation, leading to a silent decline in plant reproductive success.»

What can be inferred from  the text about light pollution?

opciones.A: It helps moths navigate more efficiently at night.
opciones.B: It has an indirect negative impact on plant reproduction.
opciones.C: It increases the daytime efficiency of honeybees.
opciones.D: It forces moths to become diurnal pollinators.

justificacion: El texto explica que la contaminación lumínica interrumpe la navegación de las polillas, lo que lleva a un declive silencioso en el éxito reproductivo de las plantas. Por lo tanto , se infiere que la luz artificial afecta indirectamente a la flora al perjudicar a sus polinizadores nocturnos.

enunciado:
Read the short dialogue and choose the best word to complete the sentence:

Sarah:  "I've been working on this code for six hours, and I still can't find the bug. I think I'm going to throw in the towel. "
Mark:  "Don't do that yet! Take a short break, get some coffee, and look at it again with fresh eyes. "

In this context, the phrase 'throw in the towel' means to  .
justificacion: La expresión idiomática 'throw in the towel' proviene del boxeo y significa rendirse o abandonar una tarea ante la frustración o el cansancio. El contexto muestra que Sarah está f rustrada tras seis horas de trabajo y Mark la anima a no rendirse ('Don't do that yet!').

enunciado:
Determine the veracity of the following statement based on the passage:

«The Mediterranean diet, rich in olive oil, whole grains, and lean proteins, has long been praised for i ts cardiovascular benefits. A new 10-year study tracking 5,000 participants confirmed that individuals who strictly adhered to this dietary pattern showed a 30% reduction in heart  disease rates compared to those on a standard Western diet, regardless of their daily physical activity levels.»

Statement: According to the study, the cardiovascular benefits o f the Mediterranean diet are only effective if the individual maintains a strict daily exercise routine.
justificacion: El enunciado afirma que los beneficios ocurren 'only if' (solo si) se mantiene una rutina de ejercicio. Sin embargo, el texto concluye explícitamente diciendo 'regardless of their  daily physical activity levels' (independientemente de sus niveles de actividad física diaria), haciendo que la afirmación sea falsa.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Biomimicry is the practice of looking to nature for inspiration to solve complex human engineering problems. A classic example is Velcro , which was invented after a Swiss engineer closely examined how burrs (plant seeds) naturally clung to his dog's fur using tiny microscopic hooks. Today, scientists are studying t he bumpy surface of whale flippers to design aerodynamic wind turbine blades that generate more electricity with less noise.»

What is the main idea of the passage?

opciones.A: Swiss engineers are the world leaders in renewable energy designs.
opciones.B: Microscopic hooks are the most efficient way to fasten clothing materials.
opciones.C: Human technology and engineering can be advanced by replicating biological designs.
opciones.D: Whale flippers are being modified by biologists to prevent ocean noise pollution.

justificacion: La idea principal del texto es definir y ejemplificar la biomímesis como el uso de diseños de la naturaleza para resolver problemas de ingeniería humana. Las opciones A, B y D mal interpretan los ejemplos específicos o generalizan de forma errónea.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The fast fashion business model relies on mass-producing trendy clothing lines at incredibly low costs. While this democratizes access t o high-end styles for average consumers, it comes with a staggering environmental toll. The industry generates roughly 10% of global carbon emissions and consumes trillions of lite rs of water annually. Furthermore, because the garments are made from cheap synthetic fibers, they degrade quickly, causing millions of tons of textiles to end up in landfills each  year.»

Based on the text, what allows fast fashion brands to make trendy styles accessible to average consumers?

opciones.A: The use of high-quality organic cotton.
opciones.B: Mass production at very low manufacturing costs.
opciones.D: A global reduction in carbon emissions.

justificacion: El texto señala textualmente en la primera oración que el modelo se basa en 'mass-producing trendy clothing lines at incredibly low costs' (producir en masa líneas de ropa de tend encia a costos increíblemente bajos), lo que directamente permite democratizar el acceso a estos estilos.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«When capturing historical events, early photographers faced technical limitations that modern smartphone users can hardly imagine. In th e mid-19th century, exposure times could take several minutes. This meant that any subject moving down a street would completely disappear from the final image, leaving bustling av enues looking entirely deserted. Only stationary objects, like buildings and statues, were crisply recorded.»

Why did busy historical streets often look empty in early photograp hs?

opciones.A: Because cities were less populated in the 19th century.
opciones.B: Because photographers asked people to clear the streets before shooting.
opciones.C: Because long exposure times failed to register moving subjects.
opciones.D: Because early cameras could only capture structures made of stone.

justificacion: El texto explica que debido a que los tiempos de exposición requerían varios minutos, cualquier sujeto en movimiento desaparecía de la imagen final ('any subject moving... would c ompletely disappear'), haciendo que las calles concurridas parecieran desiertas.

enunciado:
Read the passage and choose the best synonym for the highlighted word:

«The defense attorney argued that the evidence presented by the prosecution was  spurious , claiming it had been gathered from unreliable sources and lacked any forensic validation. He urged the jury to disregard the documents entirely.»

In this context, the word  spurious  is closest in meaning to __________ .
justificacion: La palabra 'spurious' significa falso o ilegítimo. El contexto del texto lo confirma, ya que el abogado argumenta que proviene de fuentes no confiables ('unreliable') y carece de  validación, pidiendo al jurado que lo ignore por completo.

enunciado:
Determine the veracity of the following statement based on the passage:

«While public libraries have historically been viewed simply as repositories for physical books, they ha ve quietly evolved into essential digital hubs. Today, a visitor is just as likely to use a library to access high-speed internet, learn 3D printing, or take free software programm ing courses as they are to check out a paperback novel.»

Statement: The passage suggests that modern public libraries have completely stopped offering physical books to their vi sitors.
justificacion: La afirmación dice que las bibliotecas han dejado de ofrecer libros físicos por completo ('completely stopped'). El texto solo dice que han evolucionado y que es 'igual de probabl e' ('just as likely') ir por servicios digitales como ir a pedir un libro de papel ('check out a paperback novel'), lo que significa que la oferta de libros sigue existiendo.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Deep-sea hydrothermal vents are fissures on the ocean floor that release geothermally heated water. These vents support unique ecosystem s that thrive in complete darkness under crushing pressure. Unlike land-based ecosystems that rely on sunlight via photosynthesis, deep-sea communities depend on chemosynthesis. Ba cteria use toxic chemical compounds, like hydrogen sulfide flowing from the vents, to produce energy, forming the absolute base of this deep-ocean food web.»

What serves as the  primary energy production process for ecosystems near hydrothermal vents?

opciones.A: Photosynthesis driven by deep ultraviolet light.
opciones.B: Decomposition of organic material falling from the surface.
opciones.C: Chemosynthesis carried out by specialized bacteria.
opciones.D: Geothermal radiation absorbed directly by deep-sea fish.

justificacion: El texto señala de manera explícita que, a diferencia de los ecosistemas terrestres, las comunidades de las profundidades dependen de la 'chemosynthesis' (quimiosíntesis), donde l as bacterias usan compuestos químicos para producir energía.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The concept of a 'circular economy' aims to redefine growth by focusing on positive society-wide benefits. Unlike the traditional linear  economy, which follows a 'take-make-waste' extractive model, a circular economy is restorative and regenerative by design. It relies on three main principles: designing out waste  and pollution, keeping products and materials in use, and regenerating natural systems. By transitioning to this model, industries can substantially insulate themselves from resour ce scarcity while minimizing ecological degradation.»

Which of the following describes a core principle of a circular economy according to the text?

opciones.A: Maximizing the extraction of rare raw materials.
opciones.B: Eliminating waste and pollution right from the design stage.
opciones.D: Replacing digital services with traditional physical industries.

justificacion: El texto enumera los tres principios fundamentales del modelo circular, siendo el primero 'designing out waste and pollution' (eliminar los residuos y la contaminación mediante el  diseño). La opción B refleja exactamente esta idea.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Psychologists have long studied 'confirmation bias'—the human tendency to search for, interpret, and recall information in a way that co nfirms one's preexisting beliefs. When exposed to ambiguous evidence, individuals interpret it as supporting their current stance. This cognitive bias makes constructive political  discourse exceptionally difficult, as data that contradicts a person's worldview is often subconsciously dismissed as flawed, biased, or irrelevant.»

How do individuals affected  by confirmation bias handle ambiguous data?

opciones.A: They use it to completely change their original political stance.
opciones.B: They interpret it as validation for what they already believe.
opciones.C: They seek out professional scientists to explain the data.
opciones.D: They objectively evaluate both sides of the argument.

justificacion: El fragmento dice explícitamente: 'When exposed to ambiguous evidence, individuals interpret it as supporting their current stance' (Cuando se exponen a evidencia ambigua, las per sonas la interpretan como un respaldo a su postura actual). Esto coincide directamente con la opción B.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«In the depths of the Amazon rainforest, the glass frog possesses a remarkable evolutionary trait: translucent skin on its underside. Whe n viewed from below, its internal organs, including its beating heart and digestive tract, are completely visible. Scientists discovered that this transparency acts as a form of ca mouflage. When sunlight filters through leaves, the frog's silhouette softens and blends seamlessly into its surroundings, baffling predators that hunt from underneath.»

What is  the primary evolutionary advantage of the glass frog's translucent skin?

opciones.A: It allows the frog to absorb nutrients directly from sunlight.
opciones.B: It makes it easier for scientists to monitor the frog's heart health.
opciones.C: It helps the frog blend into the background to hide from certain predators.
opciones.D: It signals to other animals that the frog is highly toxic.

justificacion: El texto indica que la transparencia actúa como un camuflaje ('acts as a form of camouflage') que suaviza su silueta frente a la luz solar, confundiendo a los depredadores que caz an desde abajo ('baffling predators that hunt from underneath').

enunciado:
Read the text and choose the best explanation for the underlined phrase:

«When Alex was offered a corporate promotion that required moving across the country, he was hesitant.  He loved his current neighborhood and friends, but the new salary was substantial. Ultimately, he decided to take the job, realizing that you  cannot make an omelet without breaking eggs .»

What did Alex mean by using the phrase  cannot make an omelet without breaking eggs ?

opciones.A: He needed to learn how to cook before moving away.
opciones.B: It is impossible to achieve something desirable without making some sacrifices.
opciones.C: Corporate promotions always lead to unexpected financial problems.
opciones.D: He wanted to break off his friendships permanently before leaving.

justificacion: El modismo 'you can't make an omelet without breaking eggs' significa que para lograr un objetivo importante o un cambio positivo, es inevitable tener que hacer sacrificios o pasa r por inconvenientes (en este caso, dejar su vecindario y amigos por una mejora profesional).

enunciado:
Determine the veracity of the following statement based on the passage:

«Graphene, a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a hexagonal lattice, is often hailed as a wonder m aterial. It is a hundred times stronger than steel, yet incredibly flexible and lightweight. Additionally, graphene conducts electricity and heat with unparalleled efficiency. Desp ite these revolutionary properties, widespread commercial adoption has stalled due to the prohibitive financial costs and technical difficulties associated with mass-producing high -quality sheets.»

Statement: According to the passage, the primary reason graphene is not widely used in consumer products yet is that the material is too fragile and breaks eas ily under pressure.
justificacion: La afirmación sostiene que no se usa por ser frágil y romperse fácil. Sin embargo, el texto dice todo lo contrario: es 'a hundred times stronger than steel' (cien veces más fuerte  que el acero). La verdadera razón de su lento despliegue comercial son los altos costos financieros y las dificultades técnicas de su producción en masa.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The Rosetta Stone, discovered in 1799 by French soldiers in Egypt, proved to be the key to unlocking ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs. Becau se the stone featured the exact same royal decree written in three distinct scripts—Hieroglyphic, Demotic, and Ancient Greek—scholars were finally able to use their knowledge of Gr eek to cross-reference and decipher the long-lost Egyptian symbols, opening a window into centuries of unread history.»

Why was the Rosetta Stone so crucial for historians?

opciones.A: It contained hidden tactical secrets about the French military strategy.
opciones.B: It provided a multilingual text that allowed scholars to translate hieroglyphs.
opciones.C: It proved that the ancient Greeks were the original rulers of Egypt.
opciones.D: It described the architectural blueprints for building the great pyramids.

justificacion: El texto aclara que la piedra tenía el mismo decreto en tres escrituras diferentes, incluyendo el griego antiguo. Esto permitió a los académicos realizar referencias cruzadas ('cr oss-reference') y descifrar los jeroglíficos utilizando su conocimiento del griego.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Urban green roofs—roofs of buildings covered with vegetation—are gaining traction in modern architecture. Beyond their aesthetic appeal,  these living structures provide immense practical utility. They act as natural insulation, lowering heating costs in winter and cooling expenses in summer. Furthermore, green roof s absorb heavy rainfall, significantly reducing the volume of stormwater runoff that overflows city sewer systems during intense storms.»

According to the passage, how do green  roofs assist a city's infrastructure during heavy storms?

opciones.A: They generate clean solar energy from the plants' leaves.
opciones.B: They block heavy winds from damaging the structural foundations.
opciones.C: They absorb rainwater, reducing the strain on urban sewer systems.
opciones.D: They automatically filter drinking water for the building's residents.

justificacion: Al final del texto se menciona explícitamente que las cubiertas verdes 'absorb heavy rainfall, significantly reducing the volume of stormwater runoff that overflows city sewer sys tems' (absorben las lluvias fuertes, reduciendo significativamente el volumen de escorrentía que desborda los alcantarillados).

enunciado:
Read the passage and choose the best synonym for the highlighted word:

«The sudden economic crisis forced many families to adopt highly  frugal  lifestyles. Extravagant vacations and dining at upscale restaurants were immediately replaced by home-cooked meals, budget spreadsheets, and thrift-store shopping to safeguard the ir dwindling savings.»

In this context, the word  frugal  is closest in meaning to ________ .
justificacion: La palabra 'frugal' se refiere a ser cauteloso, medido o ahorrativo con el dinero y los recursos. El contexto lo demuestra al mencionar que se eliminaron los lujos y se optó por c omidas caseras, presupuestos estrictos y compras de segunda mano para cuidar los ahorros.

enunciado:
Determine the veracity of the following statement based on the passage:

«For decades, space agencies have relied exclusively on multi-stage rockets that burn up or crash into t he ocean after a single launch, making space travel astronomical in cost. The introduction of partially reusable orbital rockets has dramatically flipped this paradigm. By autonomo usly landing the first-stage booster back on earth to be refurbished and flown again, launch costs have plummeted by more than 60%.»

Statement: Reusable rockets have successfull y reduced the financial cost of launching payloads into space.
justificacion: El texto señala textualmente en la última frase que al hacer aterrizar y reutilizar la primera etapa del cohete, 'launch costs have plummeted by more than 60%' (los costos de lanz amiento se han desplomado más de un 60%), validando por completo la afirmación.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«In the field of art restoration, experts face a constant ethical dilemma. When repairing an ancient masterpiece damaged by time, should  they aggressively paint over damaged sections to restore the artwork's original vibrant look, or should they apply the bare minimum stabilization to preserve the historical integri ty of what remains? An over-restored painting risks losing the genuine touch of the original master, while under-restoration can leave a piece looking fragmented and unappealing to  museum visitors.»

What is the main dilemma art restorers face according to the text?

opciones.A: Finding enough funding to purchase original 16th-century oil paints.
opciones.B: Balancing cosmetic restoration with the preservation of historical authenticity.
opciones.C: Deciding whether to sell restored masterpieces to private collectors.
opciones.D: Determining if digital replicas are superior to physical canvas paintings.

justificacion: El dilema expuesto gira en torno a si se debe pintar agresivamente para que luzca como al principio (cosmética/estética) o hacer lo mínimo indispensable para mantener la obra orig inal (autenticidad histórica). La opción B resume a la perfección este equilibrio ético.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Deep beneath the surface of the Earth, cave systems support highly specialized life forms known as troglobites. Because they live in per petual, absolute darkness, many troglobites have completely lost their eyes and skin pigments over millions of years of evolution. Instead, they have developed highly acute non-vis ual senses, such as elongated antennae and specialized chemical receptors, allowing them to navigate and hunt with astonishing precision in an environment completely devoid of ligh t.»

Based on the text, why do many troglobites lack eyes?

opciones.A: Because they are born with physical injuries caused by sharp cave rocks.
opciones.B: Because they spend half of their life cycle hunting in the sunlit surface world.
opciones.C: Because evolutionary adaptation to perpetual darkness rendered eyes unnecessary.
opciones.D: Because their eyes are hidden beneath a thick, dark layer of skin pigment.

justificacion: El texto explica que debido a que viven en oscuridad absoluta y perpetua ('perpetual, absolute darkness'), estas criaturas perdieron completamente sus ojos y pigmentación a lo lar go de millones de años de evolución, adaptándose con otros sentidos no visuales.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The introduction of remote work models has forced a reevaluation of traditional commercial real estate. Before 2020, corporations compet ed intensely for multi-story office buildings in dense urban downtown areas. Today, high vacancy rates in major cities have caused property values to decline significantly. In resp onse, urban developers are increasingly converting empty office cubicles into residential apartments and mixed-use community spaces, revitalizing neighborhoods that used to empty o ut completely after 5:00 PM.»

According to the passage, how are urban developers responding to the decline in office space demand?

opciones.A: By forcing remote employees to return to physical offices immediately.
opciones.B: By transforming vacant corporate buildings into residential and community areas.
opciones.C: By building larger multi-story office towers to attract foreign corporations.
opciones.D: By demolishing old neighborhoods to construct highways and parking structures.

justificacion: El texto establece de forma explícita que los desarrolladores urbanos están respondiendo al transformar cubículos vacíos en apartamentos residenciales y espacios comunitarios de u so mixto ('converting empty office cubicles into residential apartments and mixed-use community spaces').

enunciado:
Read the passage and choose the best synonym for the highlighted word:

«The committee praised Dr. Vance for her  impeccable  research methods. Every single dataset she included in the final medical report had been cross-checked multiple times, leaving absolutely no room for errors, omissions, or statist ical bias.»

In this context, the word  impeccable  is closest in meaning to __________ .
justificacion: La palabra 'impeccable' significa impecable, perfecto o sin defectos. El contexto del enunciado lo corrobora al señalar que los datos se revisaron varias veces, sin dejar margen p ara errores u omisiones ('leaving absolutely no room for errors'), lo que equivale a algo impecable o 'flawless'.

enunciado:
Determine the veracity of the following statement based on the passage:

«Electric vehicles (EVs) are highly effective at eliminating tailpipe emissions, which significantly imp roves air quality inside crowded cities. However, environmental scientists warn that the manufacturing process of EVs is currently far more carbon-intensive than that of convention al internal combustion vehicles. This is primarily due to the extensive mining and chemical refining required to produce high-capacity lithium-ion batteries.»

Statement: The pas sage claims that manufacturing a brand-new electric vehicle releases fewer carbon emissions than manufacturing a conventional gasoline car.
justificacion: La afirmación sostiene que la fabricación de un vehículo eléctrico libera menos emisiones que la de un carro convencional. El texto dice lo opuesto de manera explícita: 'the manuf acturing process of EVs is currently far more carbon-intensive' (el proceso de manufactura de los EVs genera actualmente mucho más carbono), debido al proceso de producción de las  baterías.

enunciado:
Read the text and choose the best explanation for the underlined phrase:

«When the small family-owned bakery opened its second branch right across the street from a major inter national supermarket chain, local analysts feared the bakery would quickly close. However, by focusing on artisanal sourdough and building personal connections with customers, the  bakery has managed to  hold its ground  and maintain a loyal clientele.»

What did the analysts mean by using the phrase  hold its ground ?

opciones.A: The bakery decided to buy the land underneath the supermarket.
opciones.B: The bakery successfully resisted the competition and maintained its position.
opciones.C: The bakery went out of business due to unexpected financial losses.
opciones.D: The bakery completely changed its product line to sell industrial groceries.

justificacion: La frase idiomática 'hold one's ground' significa mantenerse firme, defender una posición o resistir frente a una fuerza o competencia opuesta sin retroceder. En este contexto, si gnifica que la pastelería familiar resistió exitosamente la competencia del gran supermercado.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The migratory journey of the monarch butterfly is one of nature's most baffling spectacles. Every autumn, millions of monarchs fly thous ands of miles from southern Canada and the northern United States to specific oyamel fir forests in central Mexico. What makes this feat incredible is that no single butterfly ever  makes the entire round trip. It takes multiple successive generations to complete the annual cycle, meaning the insects successfully navigate to wintering sites they have never se en before, relying entirely on inherited genetic programming.»

How do monarch butterflies find their way to wintering sites in Mexico if they have never been there before?

opciones.A: By following older, experienced butterflies that lead the migration.
opciones.B: By tracking the artificial light pollution emitted from large Mexican cities.
opciones.C: By using innate genetic instructions passed down through generations.
opciones.D: By changing their diet to eat leaves from oyamel trees along the route.

justificacion: El texto aclara que ninguna mariposa realiza el viaje completo y que navegan hacia sitios que nunca han visto antes 'relying entirely on inherited genetic programming' (dependiend o enteramente de una programación genética heredada).

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Passive solar architecture focuses on designing buildings that harvest energy from natural sunlight without relying on mechanical or ele ctrical devices. For instance, a passive solar home typically features massive south-facing windows to capture thermal energy during the winter, combined with heavy concrete or bri ck interior walls known as thermal mass. These dense walls absorb heat during the sunlit hours and slowly radiate it back into the living space at night, keeping the temperature st able.»

What is the function of the 'thermal mass' inside a passive solar building?

opciones.A: It converts sunlight into high-voltage electricity for appliances.
opciones.B: It blocks all sunlight from entering the house through the windows.
opciones.C: It stores thermal energy during the day and releases it during the night.
opciones.D: It pumps cold water through the roof to reduce cooling costs.

justificacion: Según el fragmento, las paredes densas que actúan como masa térmica 'absorb heat during the sunlit hours and slowly radiate it back into the living space at night' (absorben calor  durante las horas de sol y lo irradian lentamente al espacio habitable por la noche), estabilizando la temperatura.

enunciado:
Read the passage and choose the best synonym for the highlighted word:

«The long-standing border dispute between the two neighboring countries had been a source of regional ten sion for decades. However, after weeks of intensive diplomatic talks, a  congenial  agreement was reached, allowing both nations to open joint trading ports and foster peaceful cultural exchanges.»

In this context, the word  congenial  is closest in meaning to ________ .
justificacion: La palabra 'congenial' denota algo agradable, amigable, compatible o armónico. El contexto del texto muestra que tras las charlas diplomáticas se pasó de una situación de tensión  a un acuerdo que fomenta puertos comerciales conjuntos y relaciones pacíficas, indicando un acuerdo armonioso ('harmonious').

enunciado:
Determine the veracity of the following statement based on the passage:

«The practice of 'forest bathing' (Shinrin-yoku), which originated in Japan during the 1980s, involves s pending quiet, contemplative time walking among trees. Clinical trials have demonstrated that physiological stress biomarkers, such as salivary cortisol levels, decrease significan tly after just forty minutes of immersion in a forest environment. Interestingly, identical walking routines conducted in urban concrete environments showed no such biological stre ss reductions.»

Statement: According to the passage, walking in a city park made of concrete provides the exact same reduction in cortisol stress levels as walking in a natural  forest.
justificacion: La afirmación indica que caminar en entornos de concreto da la misma reducción de estrés que el bosque. El texto lo desmiente al final con rotundidad: 'urban concrete environments  showed no such biological stress reductions' (los entornos urbanos de concreto no mostraron tales reducciones de estrés biológico).

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Microplastics—plastic particles smaller than five millimeters—have successfully infiltrated every major ecosystem on the planet. Origina ting from the fragmentation of synthetic textiles, commercial fishing nets, and degrading consumer trash, these particles are now routinely found in high-altitude mountain snow, re mote oceanic trenches, and inside the digestive tracts of marine animals. The primary concern among ecotoxicologists is that microplastics act as chemical sponges, absorbing organi c pollutants from surrounding waters and introducing them directly into the global food chain.»

What is the primary concern regarding microplastics according to ecotoxicologists ?

opciones.A: They completely block the sunlight required for marine photosynthesis.
opciones.B: They accumulate toxic pollutants and transport them into the food chain.
opciones.C: They freeze inside mountain glaciers, accelerating global warming.
opciones.D: They are highly visible and ruin the aesthetic beauty of coastal beaches.

justificacion: El final del texto señala que la mayor preocupación es que las partículas actúan como esponjas químicas, absorbente de contaminantes orgánicos ('absorbing organic pollutants') e i ntroduciéndolos directamente en la cadena alimenticia global ('introducing them directly into the global food chain').

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«NOTICE: DO NOT FEED THE ANIMALS. VIOLATORS WILL BE SUBJECT TO A FINE.»

opciones.A: You must pay a fee if you want to give food to the animals.
opciones.B: You are not allowed to give food to the animals, or you will have to pay money as a penalty.
opciones.C: You should bring your own food because the animals do not have enough.
opciones.D: You will get money if you help workers feed the animals in this area.

justificacion: El aviso prohíbe alimentar a los animales ('DO NOT FEED') y advierte que quienes lo hagan recibirán una multa ('SUBJECT TO A FINE'). La opción B parafrasea correctamente que está  prohibido darles comida bajo penalización económica.

enunciado:
Read the definition and choose the word that matches it:

«A building or room where objects of historical, scientific, artistic, or cultural interest are stored and exhibited to  the public.»
justificacion: La definición describe un lugar donde se guardan y exhiben objetos de interés histórico, artístico o cultural para el público, lo cual corresponde exactamente a un museo ('Museum' ). Una biblioteca guarda libros, un teatro presenta obras y un laboratorio realiza experimentos.

enunciado:
Complete the conversation with the most suitable response:

Mark:  "Hey, are you coming to the concert tonight? "
Lucy:  "______________________ "

opciones.A: I think it was very loud.
opciones.B: I'd love to, but I have to study for my finals.
opciones.C: Yes, I watched it on TV yesterday.
opciones.D: No, it doesn't open at 9:00 PM.

justificacion: Mark hace una pregunta en futuro sobre ir a un concierto esta noche ('tonight'). La opción B responde adecuadamente expresando deseo pero indicando una excusa válida en tiempo fut uro/presente. Las opciones A y C están en pasado, y la D no responde con lógica a la invitación.

enunciado:
Choose the correct word to complete the blank space in the text:

«Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world. Thousands of climbers try to reach its summit every year,  ________ only a few manage to succeed due to the extreme weather conditions.»
justificacion: El texto presenta un contraste: muchos intentan subir, pero solo unos pocos lo logran. El conector adversativo adecuado para indicar oposición o contraste entre dos ideas es 'but'  (pero).

enunciado:
Choose the correct verb form to complete the blank space:

«By the time the rescue team arrived at the remote cabin, the professional hikers ________ there for more than three d ays without any food supplies.»
justificacion: La acción de esperar ocurrió y continuó antes de otro punto en el pasado ('By the time the rescue team arrived'). Por lo tanto, se requiere el pasado perfecto continuo ('had been  waiting') para denotar la anterioridad y duración de la acción respecto al pasado.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«When the laboratory results came back, Dr. Jenkins stared at the graph in disbelief. The enzyme levels, which were supposed to drop dras tically under the new chemical treatment, remained entirely unchanged. He sighed, rubbed his eyes, and started cleaning his test tubes to prepare a completely new formula from scra tch.»

What can be inferred about Dr. Jenkins' experiment?

opciones.A: It was a major success that confirmed his original hypothesis.
opciones.B: It failed to produce the expected results, forcing him to restart.
opciones.C: It was interrupted by a dangerous chemical explosion in the lab.
opciones.D: It proved that enzymes are useless in medical treatments.

justificacion: El texto menciona que los niveles de enzimas no cambiaron como se esperaba ('remained entirely unchanged') y que el doctor tuvo que preparar una fórmula nueva desde cero ('from sc ratch'). Esto permite inferir que el experimento falló y requirió reiniciar el proceso.

enunciado:
Choose the correct preposition to complete the sentence:

«The success of the environmental project depends heavily ________ the cooperation between local citizens and governmen tal agencies.»
justificacion: En inglés, el verbo 'depend' requiere estrictamente la preposición 'on' (o 'upon') para indicar dependencia de algo o alguien ('depend on'). Las demás preposiciones son incorrecta s en esta combinación fija (phrasal verb / dependent preposition).

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«FLIGHT DELAYS EXPECTED DUE TO HEAVY FOG. PLEASE CHECK THE BOARDS FOR UPDATED DEPARTURE TIMES.»

opciones.A: All flights have been permanently canceled because of the bad weather.
opciones.B: Passengers must look at the screens to see changes in their flight schedules.
opciones.C: The airport is closed and passengers should return to their hotels.
opciones.D: Flights are leaving earlier than scheduled to avoid the morning fog.

justificacion: El aviso advierte sobre retrasos por niebla y pide revisar las pantallas ('boards') para ver los horarios actualizados ('updated departure times'). La opción B traduce esta instru cción de manera exacta.

enunciado:
Choose the correct phrase to complete the conditional sentence:

«If the government had invested more resources into public infrastructure ten years ago, the city ________ such  severe traffic gridlocks today.»
justificacion: Se trata de una condicional mixta (Mixed Conditional). La condición se refiere al pasado ('If the government had invested...'), pero el resultado afecta al presente activo ('today '). Por lo tanto, la estructura correcta para el resultado presente es 'would not be experiencing' (no estaría experimentando).

enunciado:
Read the text and choose the best word to complete the blank space:

«The company's profits plummeted this quarter. As a result, the management had to make the difficult decisio n to ________ several projects to prevent total bankruptcy.»
justificacion: El contexto indica que las ganancias cayeron en picada ('plummeted') y que se busca evitar la bancarrota ('prevent total bankruptcy'). Por ende, la acción lógica y necesaria de la  gerencia es suspender o eliminar proyectos ('cancel'). Las demás opciones empeorarían la situación financiera o contradicen el contexto.

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«MUSEUM NOTICE: FLASH PHOTOGRAPHY PROHIBITED. INTENSE LIGHT DAMAGES HISTORICAL TEXTILES.»

opciones.A: You can take pictures with flash if you pay an extra fee.
opciones.B: Taking photos with flash is forbidden because it ruins delicate old fabrics.
opciones.C: You must buy a camera inside the museum to take pictures of the clothes.
opciones.D: The museum will turn off all the lights to protect the exhibits.

justificacion: El aviso prohíbe el uso de flash ('FLASH PHOTOGRAPHY PROHIBITED') debido a que la luz intensa daña los textiles antiguos ('INTENSE LIGHT DAMAGES HISTORICAL TEXTILES'). La opción B  expresa exactamente esta restricción y su causa.

enunciado:
Read the definition and choose the word that matches it:

«A severe shortage of food in a region, which causes widespread hunger, malnutrition, and increased mortality rates amo ng the population.»
justificacion: La definición describe una escasez severa de alimentos que provoca hambre generalizada y muertes, lo cual corresponde al término 'Famine' (hambruna). 'Drought' es sequía, 'Flood'  es inundación y 'Epidemic' es epidemia.

enunciado:
Complete the conversation with the most suitable response:

Tom:  "I am extremely sorry for missing our project alignment meeting yesterday morning. "
Jane:  "____________________ "

opciones.A: Don't worry about it, but make sure to read the meeting minutes.
opciones.B: Yes, I will attend the next one without fail.
opciones.C: I think the meeting is starting right now.
opciones.D: No, I didn't see you there at all.

justificacion: Tom se disculpa por faltar a una reunión pasada ('yesterday morning'). La respuesta lógica de Jane es aceptar la disculpa amablemente y darle una instrucción de seguimiento relaci onada ('Don't worry about it, but make sure to read the meeting minutes').

enunciado:
Choose the correct word to complete the blank space in the text:

«Renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind power, are essential for combating climate change. ____ , they are completely inexhaustible, unlike traditional fossil fuels.»
justificacion: El texto añade una ventaja adicional de las energías renovables (que son inagotables) a la idea inicial (que son esenciales contra el cambio climático). El conector de adición apr opiado para introducir información de refuerzo es 'Furthermore' (además).

enunciado:
Choose the correct phrase to complete the sentence in its passive voice form:

«The global carbon emissions report ________ by the international environmental committee before t he summit began next week.»
justificacion: Se describe una acción que recae sobre el objeto (el reporte) y que ocurrió con anterioridad a otro evento en el pasado ('before the summit began'). Por ende, requiere la estructu ra del pasado perfecto en voz pasiva: 'had been + participio' ('had been published').

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) is located at the second Lagrange point (L2), approximately 1.5 million kilometers away from Earth . This strategic position allows the telescope to remain in line with the Earth as it moves around the Sun, protecting its sensitive infrared instruments from the intense thermal r adiation of both celestial bodies.»

Why is the James Webb Space Telescope positioned at the L2 point?

opciones.A: To be closer to the moon's atmosphere for quick physical repairs.
opciones.B: To shield its infrared instruments from the thermal heat of the Sun and Earth.
opciones.C: Because it is the only place in outer space where satellite radio signals work.
opciones.D: To allow astronauts to visit the telescope on a daily basis.

justificacion: El texto afirma directamente que su posición estratégica sirve para proteger sus instrumentos infrarrojos de la radiación térmica intensa del Sol y de la Tierra ('protecting its s ensitive infrared instruments from the intense thermal radiation of both celestial bodies').

enunciado:
Choose the correct preposition to complete the sentence:

«The financial director succeeded ________ persuading the board of trustees to approve the annual budget increase.»
justificacion: En inglés, el verbo 'succeed' se acopla de manera fija con la preposición 'in' seguido de un gerundio ('succeed in doing something') para expresar que se tuvo éxito al realizar un a actividad.

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«STORE POLICY: MERCHANDISE CAN ONLY BE RETURNED WITHIN 14 DAYS OF PURCHASE. ORIGINAL RECEIPT REQUIRED.»

opciones.A: You can get a refund at any time if you bring the product back.
opciones.B: Returns are accepted only if you have the receipt and it has been less than two weeks since the purchase.
opciones.C: The store will give you cash if you find someone else's old receipt.
opciones.D: You cannot buy items unless you bring an old product to exchange.

justificacion: La política restringe las devoluciones a un máximo de 14 días (dos semanas) y exige el recibo original. La opción B parafrasea de manera exacta ambas condiciones.

enunciado:
Choose the correct phrase to complete the sentence introducing negative inversion:

«Seldom ________ such a brilliant performance by a young classical pianist in a prestigious i nternational tournament.»
justificacion: Cuando una oración comienza con un adverbio negativo o restrictivo como 'Seldom' (rara vez), se debe aplicar una inversión gramatical colocando el verbo auxiliar o modal antes del  sujeto. Por lo tanto, la estructura correcta es 'has the audience witnessed'.

enunciado:
Read the text and choose the best word to complete the blank space:

«The structural integrity of the old suspension bridge was severely compromised. Therefore, the city engineers decided to ________ vehicle traffic across it until reinforcements were installed.»
justificacion: Dado que la integridad estructural del puente está comprometida ('severely compromised'), la medida inmediata y segura es detener o pausar el tránsito vehicular ('suspend'). Las d emás opciones pondrían en peligro el puente o no se corresponden con la lógica del contexto técnico.

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«PLEASE KEEP OFF THE GRASS. NEWLY PLANTED SEEDS.»

opciones.A: You are allowed to walk on the grass if you walk slowly.
opciones.B: You must not walk on the lawn because they have just planted seeds.
opciones.C: You need to bring your own seeds to plant in this garden.
opciones.D: You should help the gardeners water the newly planted seeds.

justificacion: La frase 'KEEP OFF THE GRASS' es una instrucción directa para no pisar ni caminar sobre el césped. La justificación dada en el aviso es que hay semillas recién plantadas ('NEWLY P LANTED SEEDS'). La opción B refleja correctamente esta prohibición y su razón.

enunciado:
Read the definition and choose the word that matches it:

«A professional who designs buildings and in many cases also supervises their construction.»
justificacion: La definición habla de una persona que diseña edificios ('designs buildings') y supervisa su construcción. Este es el rol exacto de un arquitecto ('Architect'). 'Plumber' es plome ro, 'Carpenter' es carpintero y 'Surgeon' es cirujano.

enunciado:
Complete the conversation with the most suitable response:

Waiter:  "Are you ready to order, or do you need a few more minutes? "
Customer:  " "

opciones.A: Yes, I will pay the bill in cash, please.
opciones.B: I think I am ready. I'll have the grilled salmon.
opciones.C: No, the food was absolutely delicious.
opciones.D: I'm sorry, I don't work at this restaurant.

justificacion: El mesero pregunta si el cliente está listo para ordenar comida ('ready to order'). La opción B es la única respuesta lógica en la que el cliente confirma que está listo y procede  a pedir su platillo ('I'll have the grilled salmon').

enunciado:
Choose the correct word to complete the blank space in the text:

«  the heavy rain and strong winds, the football match continued until the final whistle without any interruptions.»
justificacion: El texto presenta un contraste: había lluvia y viento, pero el partido continuó. Como la frase introductoria es una frase nominal ('the heavy rain and strong winds') y no contiene  un verbo conjugado, se debe usar la preposición 'Despite' (A pesar de). 'Although' requeriría una oración completa con sujeto y verbo.

enunciado:
Choose the correct verb form to complete the blank space:

«Sarah is completely exhausted right now because she ________ for the upcoming marathon since 5:00 AM.»
justificacion: La acción de correr comenzó en el pasado ('since 5:00 AM') y continúa en el presente, causando un efecto visible ahora ('is completely exhausted right now'). Esto requiere el uso  del presente perfecto continuo: 'has been running'.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«As soon as Mr. Harrison walked into the classroom, the students quickly put their phones away, sat up straight, and opened their textboo ks. The previous loud chatter completely vanished, leaving only the sound of turning pages.»

What can be inferred about the classroom environment?

opciones.A: Mr. Harrison is a new student trying to make friends.
opciones.B: The students are getting ready to leave for a field trip.
opciones.C: Mr. Harrison commands respect and maintains strict discipline.
opciones.D: The students' phones stopped working simultaneously.

justificacion: La reacción inmediata de los estudiantes (guardar los teléfonos, sentarse derechos y hacer silencio total) al entrar el profesor demuestra que hay un ambiente de gran disciplina,  autoridad y respeto hacia él. La opción C es la única inferencia lógica basada en este comportamiento.

enunciado:
Choose the correct preposition to complete the sentence:

«If you don't know the exact meaning of a complicated word, you should look it ________ in an online dictionary.»
justificacion: El phrasal verb correcto para buscar información, como el significado de una palabra en un diccionario o referencia, es 'look up' (If the object is a pronoun like 'it', it goes in  the middle: 'look it up'). 'Look for' significa buscar un objeto perdido, y 'look after' significa cuidar a alguien.

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«CAUTION: WET FLOOR. CLEANING IN PROGRESS. PLEASE USE ALTERNATIVE ROUTE.»

opciones.A: You must clean the floor before walking here.
opciones.B: The floor is slippery, so it is recommended to walk another way.
opciones.C: The cleaning staff is currently on break.
opciones.D: You cannot leave the building until the floor is completely dry.

justificacion: El aviso advierte precaución ('CAUTION') por un piso mojado ('WET FLOOR') y pide usar una ruta alterna ('USE ALTERNATIVE ROUTE'). La opción B transmite este mensaje de seguridad d e manera clara y precisa.

enunciado:
Choose the correct phrase to complete the sentence in Reported Speech:

Direct speech:  "I will submit the final report tomorrow, " Mary said.
Reported speech: Mary said that she ________ the final report the next day.
justificacion: Al cambiar de estilo directo a indirecto (Reported Speech) cuando el verbo introductorio está en pasado ('said'), el verbo de la oración original debe retroceder un tiempo verbal  (Backshift). 'Will' cambia a su forma pasada condicional 'would'. Por lo tanto, 'would submit' es correcto.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«Are you tired of paying ridiculously high electricity bills? Switch to SolarMax panels today! Our cutting-edge technology will reduce yo ur energy costs by 50% in just the first month. Call our toll-free number right now to get a completely free consultation!»

What is the main purpose of this text?

opciones.A: To explain the scientific process of how solar panels are manufactured.
opciones.B: To warn local citizens about the physical dangers of electricity.
opciones.C: To persuade consumers to purchase a specific brand of solar panels.
opciones.D: To report on recent government policies regarding climate change.

justificacion: El texto utiliza un lenguaje netamente publicitario y persuasivo ('Are you tired of...', 'Switch to...', 'Call our toll-free number right now...'). Su objetivo principal es vender  un producto (paneles SolarMax), lo que corresponde a la opción C.

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«OFFICE HOURS: MONDAY TO FRIDAY, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM. STAFF AVAILABLE BY APPOINTMENT ONLY ON SATURDAYS.»

opciones.A: The office is open to everyone every single day of the week.
opciones.B: On Saturdays, you need to book a time in advance if you want to see someone.
opciones.C: Staff members do not work at all during the weekdays.
opciones.D: You can visit the office on Saturdays without notifying anyone.

justificacion: El aviso indica que los sábados el personal está disponible solo bajo cita previa ('BY APPOINTMENT ONLY'). Esto significa que se debe reservar un tiempo con antelación.

enunciado:
Read the definition and choose the word that matches it:

«An official document that allows you to travel to, or live in, a foreign country.»
justificacion: Una 'Visa' es el documento oficial otorgado por un país que autoriza a una persona a entrar o residir temporalmente en dicho territorio. El pasaporte identifica a la persona, el b oleto permite el transporte y la moneda es el dinero.

enunciado:
Complete the conversation with the most suitable response:

Anna:  "I've been feeling under the weather all week. Do you think I should see a doctor? "
Ben:  " ____________________ "

opciones.A: Yes, it's definitely better to be safe than sorry.
opciones.B: No, I think you should buy a new car today.
opciones.C: I agree, the weather in this city is very strange.
opciones.D: No, let's go to the cinema instead.

justificacion: Anna dice sentirse 'under the weather' (un modismo para sentirse mal de salud). La opción A es la única respuesta lógica, ofreciendo un consejo médico prudente.

enunciado:
Choose the correct word to complete the blank space in the text:

«The new public park was ________ designed to include wheelchair-accessible pathways, ensuring that everyone in  the community can enjoy the space.»
justificacion: Se necesita un adverbio para modificar al verbo 'designed' (diseñado). 'Carefully' es el adverbio que describe cómo se hizo la acción de diseñar.

enunciado:
Choose the correct phrase to complete the sentence:

«In my opinion, the second movie of the trilogy is ________ the first one, but the final part is definitely the best.»
justificacion: Para comparar dos cosas (la segunda película y la primera), se requiere la estructura de comparativo de superioridad: 'más que' ('more interesting than').

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«To reduce operational waste, the coffee shop has implemented a 'bring your own mug' policy. Customers who present their own reusable con tainer receive a 10% discount on their beverage purchase. This initiative has not only decreased the consumption of disposable paper cups by 30% but has also fostered a stronger se nse of environmental responsibility among regular customers.»

What is the benefit for customers who bring their own mugs?

opciones.A: They get a free coffee after every ten visits.
opciones.B: They receive a 10% discount on their drinks.
opciones.C: They are allowed to enter the staff-only kitchen area.
opciones.D: They can skip the line and be served first.

justificacion: El texto establece explícitamente: 'Customers who present their own reusable container receive a 10% discount on their beverage purchase'.

enunciado:
Choose the correct preposition to complete the sentence:

«He is really interested ________ learning how to code in Python during the summer break.»
justificacion: El adjetivo 'interested' se usa siempre con la preposición 'in' cuando se expresa interés en realizar o aprender algo ('interested in doing something').

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose the option that best describes its meaning:

«EMERGENCY EXIT: KEEP CLEAR AT ALL TIMES. DO NOT BLOCK WITH BOXES OR EQUIPMENT.»

opciones.A: You can leave your heavy boxes here temporarily.
opciones.B: This door is only for emergencies and must remain unobstructed.
opciones.C: Please use this exit to throw away trash and old boxes.
opciones.D: You need a special key to open this door during the day.

justificacion: El aviso indica que la salida de emergencia debe permanecer libre ('keep clear') y no debe bloquearse con objetos. La opción B captura la esencia de la necesidad de mantener el ac ceso libre para emergencias.

enunciado:
Choose the correct modal verb to complete the sentence:

«You ________ study hard if you want to pass the certification exam, as it is known to be very challenging.»
justificacion: Dado que el examen es muy difícil ('very challenging'), se requiere una obligación o necesidad fuerte para pasarlo. 'Must' es el modal adecuado para expresar esa necesidad imperat iva.

enunciado:
Read the text and answer the question:

«The factory was once the heart of the small town, employing nearly every resident. When it closed down in 2005, the local economy wither ed, shops shut their doors, and many families moved away in search of work. Today, the town is attempting to reinvent itself as a destination for eco-tourism.»

What happened to  the town after the factory closed?

opciones.A: It became the most popular tourist city in the country.
opciones.B: It experienced a significant economic downturn and population loss.
opciones.C: It built a new, more modern factory that created more jobs.
opciones.D: It remained exactly the same as it was before 2005.

justificacion: El texto describe que la economía local 'withered' (se marchitó), los negocios cerraron y las familias se mudaron. Esto equivale a un declive económico y pérdida de población.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«By the time we ________ to the theater, the play ________ already started.»
justificacion: Se utiliza el pasado simple ('arrived') para la acción más reciente y el pasado perfecto ('had started') para la acción que ocurrió antes, cumpliendo con la estructura de anterior idad en el pasado.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«You ________ smoke inside the hospital; it is strictly prohibited by law.»
justificacion: 'Must not' se utiliza para expresar una prohibición fuerte o algo que va en contra de una regla o ley. 'Don't have to' indicaría falta de obligación, lo cual no es el caso.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«If I ________ more time, I ________ you with that project last week.»
justificacion: Es una condicional de tercer tipo (hipótesis sobre el pasado). La estructura requiere 'If + past perfect' y 'would have + participio' en el resultado.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most appropriate connector:

«The company is facing financial difficulties; ______ , they have decided to cut down on unnecessary expenses.»
justificacion: 'Therefore' (por lo tanto) establece una relación de causa-efecto lógica entre las dificultades financieras y la decisión de recortar gastos.

enunciado:
Choose the correct passive voice form to complete the sentence:

«The new bridge ________ by the mayor next Friday.»
justificacion: La mención de 'next Friday' indica futuro, por lo que la voz pasiva requiere el auxiliar futuro 'will be' seguido del participio 'inaugurated'.

competencia: Competencia Gramatical (Formas verbales: Gerundio vs Infinitivo)
enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I remember ________ that movie when I was a child, but I can't recall the title.»
justificacion: El verbo 'remember' seguido de un gerundio ('seeing') indica recordar haber hecho algo en el pasado. 'Remember to see' significaría recordar que uno debe realizar la acción en el  futuro.

enunciado:
Complete the text with the correct word:

«After hours of debate, the committee finally reached a ________ regarding the new environmental policy.»
justificacion: 'Consensus' es el término adecuado para un acuerdo al que llega un grupo tras discutir. Las otras palabras denotan conflicto o falta de atención.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I have ________ information about the flight, but I need ________ more details from the airline.»
justificacion: Information es incontable, por lo que se usa 'some' para oraciones afirmativas en ambos casos. 'Any' se usa generalmente en preguntas o negaciones.

enunciado:
Choose the correct relative pronoun to complete the sentence:

«The scientist ________ discovered the cure for the disease was awarded a Nobel Prize.»
justificacion: 'Who' es el pronombre relativo necesario para referirse a una persona (el científico) que actúa como sujeto de la acción siguiente ('discovered').

enunciado:
Choose the correct word to complete the sentence:

«The suspect managed to ________ the police by hiding in the forest during the night.»
justificacion: 'Elude' significa escapar o evitar a alguien con astucia, lo cual encaja con el contexto de esconderse de la policía.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«She ________ for the bus when it suddenly started to rain.»
justificacion: Se utiliza el pasado continuo ('was waiting') para describir una acción que estaba en progreso cuando otra acción puntual en pasado ('started') la interrumpió.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I ________ finish this report by tomorrow morning, otherwise, the manager will be furious.»
justificacion: 'Must' se utiliza aquí para indicar una necesidad u obligación fuerte, subrayada por la consecuencia negativa de no hacerlo.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«If you ________ your homework, you ________ TV right now.»
justificacion: Se trata de una condicional mixta (tipo 2): 'If + past simple' seguido de un resultado presente continuo ('would be watching'), indicando un estado actual hipotético.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most appropriate connector:

«He didn't study for the exam; ____ , he passed with flying colors.»
justificacion: 'However' (sin embargo) introduce un contraste inesperado entre no estudiar y aun así obtener una excelente calificación.

enunciado:
Choose the correct passive voice form to complete the sentence:

«The office ________ while we were away on vacation.»
justificacion: La acción ocurrió en un tiempo pasado definido ('while we were away'), por lo que se requiere el pasado simple en voz pasiva: 'was + participio'.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I stopped ________ to my friend because I needed to concentrate on the exam.»
justificacion: 'Stop' seguido de gerundio ('talking') indica finalizar una actividad que se estaba realizando. 'Stop to talk' significaría detenerse para comenzar a hablar.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most suitable adjective:

«It was a very ________ situation when everyone started laughing during the formal ceremony.»
justificacion: 'Awkward' describe una situación incómoda o embarazosa, que es lo que ocurre cuando alguien rompe la formalidad con risas inapropiadas.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«There isn't ________ milk left in the fridge, so we should go to the store.»
justificacion: En oraciones negativas e interrogativas con sustantivos incontables, se utiliza 'any'.

enunciado:
Choose the correct pronoun to complete the sentence:

«The dog wagged ________ tail when it saw its owner arriving.»
justificacion: 'Its' es el adjetivo posesivo para cosas o animales en tercera persona singular.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct verb:

«If you want to keep your job, you must ________ by the company's new rules.»
justificacion: El phrasal verb 'abide by' significa cumplir, respetar o seguir unas reglas o normas.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«She ________ as a teacher for ten years before she decided to switch careers.»
justificacion: Se requiere el pasado perfecto continuo ('had been working') porque la acción continuó hasta un punto en el pasado ('before she decided').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I didn't hear the alarm, so I ________ have slept through it.»
justificacion: 'Must have' se utiliza para hacer una deducción lógica fuerte sobre algo que sucedió en el pasado.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«If you heat ice, it ______ .»
justificacion: Se trata de la 'Zero Conditional', utilizada para verdades universales o hechos científicos, usando presente simple en ambas partes.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most appropriate connector:

«I studied hard for the test; ____ , I didn't get the grade I expected.»
justificacion: 'Nevertheless' (no obstante) introduce una concesión, contrastando el esfuerzo realizado con el resultado obtenido.

enunciado:
Choose the correct passive voice form to complete the sentence:

«The masterpiece ________ by a famous artist in the 19th century.»
justificacion: Se indica un momento específico en el pasado ('in the 19th century'), requiriendo pasado simple en voz pasiva ('was + participio').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«She suggested ________ to the cinema, but I was too tired.»
justificacion: El verbo 'suggest' siempre debe ir seguido de un gerundio (-ing) cuando se propone una acción.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct word:

«The candidate's ________ speech impressed the audience and secured him the victory.»
justificacion: 'Eloquent' (elocuente) describe una forma de hablar fluida, elegante y persuasiva, lo cual explica por qué impresionó a la audiencia.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«How ________ time do we have before the train leaves?»
justificacion: Se usa 'much' con sustantivos incontables como 'time'.

enunciado:
Choose the correct pronoun to complete the sentence:

«They finished the project by ________ without any outside help.»
justificacion: 'Themselves' es el pronombre reflexivo correcto para enfatizar que ellos realizaron la acción por cuenta propia.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct phrasal verb:

«The flight was ________ because of the storm, so we had to wait at the airport.»
justificacion: 'Called off' significa cancelar un evento o, en este contexto, suspender un servicio debido a causas externas.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«By the end of next month, I ________ this project for two years.»
justificacion: Se utiliza el futuro perfecto continuo ('will have been working') para indicar la duración de una acción hasta un punto determinado en el futuro.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«You ________ have seen John at the party because he is currently on vacation in France.»
justificacion: 'Can't have' se usa para hacer una deducción lógica negativa sobre un hecho en el pasado, concluyendo que algo es imposible.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«If you ________ to the lecture, you would have understood the assignment better.»
justificacion: Corresponde a una condicional de tercer tipo, la cual plantea una situación hipotética en el pasado. Se requiere 'had + participio' en la condición.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most appropriate connector:

«The company decided to expand into new markets; ______ , they are hiring more sales staff.»
justificacion: 'Consequently' (por consiguiente/como resultado) expresa la relación directa de causa-efecto entre la expansión y la contratación de personal.

enunciado:
Choose the correct passive voice form to complete the sentence:

«Many scientific articles ________ in this journal every year.»
justificacion: La presencia de 'every year' indica un hecho presente habitual, por lo que se requiere el presente simple en voz pasiva ('are + participio').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«He avoided ________ the question during the interview.»
justificacion: El verbo 'avoid' (evitar) requiere obligatoriamente ir seguido de un gerundio (-ing) para indicar la actividad que se está evitando.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct word:

«The CEO's ________ approach to problem-solving helped the company overcome the crisis efficiently.»
justificacion: 'Pragmatic' (pragmático) se refiere a un enfoque práctico y realista, que es ideal para resolver crisis de manera eficiente.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I have ________ friends living in London, so I always have someone to visit.»
justificacion: Se usa 'a few' para sustantivos contables en plural cuando queremos indicar una cantidad pequeña pero existente.

enunciado:
Choose the correct relative pronoun to complete the sentence:

«The book ________ you lent me is absolutely fascinating.»
justificacion: 'Which' se utiliza para referirse a cosas u objetos (el libro) en una oración subordinada.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct verb:

«I need to ________ up on my French skills before the trip to Paris.»
justificacion: 'Brush up on' es un phrasal verb que significa repasar o mejorar una habilidad que se tenía olvidada.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«By the time the regional manager arrived at the office, the team ________ the quarterly presentation.»
justificacion: Se utiliza el pasado perfecto ('had finished') para indicar que una acción se completó en el pasado antes de que ocurriera otra acción pasada ('arrived').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«You ________ have spoken to the client like that; it was very unprofessional and could cost us the contract.»
justificacion: La estructura 'shouldn't have' seguida de un participio pasado se usa para expresar una crítica o arrepentimiento sobre algo que se hizo en el pasado y que fue una mala idea o ina propiado.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the conditional sentence:

«If the company ________ the new marketing strategy, profits will likely increase by the end of the year.»
justificacion: Es una condicional de primer tipo (First Conditional), que se utiliza para situaciones futuras probables. Requiere la estructura 'If + presente simple' en la cláusula condicional  y futuro ('will') en el resultado.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most appropriate connector:

«The new software is highly secure against cyber attacks;  , it is extremely intuitive and user-friendly.»
justificacion: 'Furthermore' (además) se utiliza para añadir información positiva adicional a un punto que ya se ha mencionado, reforzando las ventajas del software.

enunciado:
Choose the correct passive voice form to complete the sentence:

«A massive new shopping mall ________ in the city center right now, and it is expected to open next spring.»
justificacion: La frase 'right now' indica una acción en progreso en el presente. Por lo tanto, se necesita el presente continuo en voz pasiva: 'is being + participio' ('is being built').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«The entire board of directors is looking forward to ________ you at the annual conference next week.»
justificacion: La expresión idiomática 'look forward to' (esperar con ansias) siempre va seguida de un sustantivo o un gerundio (-ing), ya que la palabra 'to' en este caso funciona como preposic ión y no como parte de un infinitivo.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct word:

«The accountant was incredibly ________ in checking the financial records, ensuring that not a single penny was unaccounted for.»
justificacion: 'Meticulous' describe a alguien que presta extrema atención a los detalles de manera cuidadosa y precisa, lo cual encaja perfectamente con revisar registros financieros sin perder  un centavo.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«  of the two candidates seemed qualified enough for the demanding engineering role, so the company decided to keep searching.»
justificacion: Se utiliza 'Neither' para indicar que 'ninguno de los dos' cumple con la condición. El contexto muestra que la empresa siguió buscando, lo que implica que los dos candidatos previ os fueron rechazados.

enunciado:
Choose the correct relative pronoun to complete the sentence:

«The renowned professor, ________ research focuses entirely on renewable energy, won an international sustainabili ty award yesterday.»
justificacion: Se necesita el pronombre relativo posesivo 'whose' (cuyo/cuya) para indicar que la investigación (research) pertenece al profesor (professor).

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct phrasal verb:

«We need to find a gas station immediately; the warning light is on and we are ________ fuel.»
justificacion: El phrasal verb 'run out of' significa quedarse sin algo (agotarse un recurso). Las otras opciones significan compensar (make up for), salir impune (get away with) y menospreciar  (look down on).

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«By the time you finish reading this book, you ________ everything there is to know about the topic.»
justificacion: Se utiliza el futuro perfecto ('will have known') para hablar de un estado o conocimiento que estará completo en un momento futuro determinado ('by the time...').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I ________ have finished the project earlier, but I had too many other tasks to handle.»
justificacion: 'Could have' se utiliza para expresar una posibilidad o habilidad pasada que no se realizó ('podría haber terminado').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the conditional sentence:

«If you were in my position, what ________ you do?»
justificacion: Se trata de una segunda condicional (hipótesis irreal o hipotética en el presente), que utiliza pasado simple en la condición y 'would' en el resultado.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the most appropriate connector:

«The train was delayed due to technical issues; ____ , we arrived at the station an hour later than expected.»
justificacion: 'Consequently' (en consecuencia) es el conector que mejor expresa el resultado directo de la demora del tren.

enunciado:
Choose the correct passive voice form to complete the sentence:

«The documents ________ to the office before the deadline yesterday.»
justificacion: Se requiere el pasado simple en voz pasiva ('were sent') para indicar una acción concluida en un momento pasado específico ('yesterday').

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«I regret ________ you that the position has already been filled.»
justificacion: 'Regret' seguido de infinitivo ('to inform') se usa formalmente para dar malas noticias, mientras que 'regret' + gerundio significaría arrepentirse de algo que se hizo.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct word:

«The artist's work is ________ unique, as it combines traditional techniques with modern digital tools.»
justificacion: 'Highly' funciona aquí como un adverbio de grado para enfatizar la naturaleza única ('unique') del trabajo artístico.

enunciado:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:

«Unfortunately, there was ________ interest in the project, so it was canceled.»
justificacion: Se usa 'little' (con sentido negativo, casi nada) para sustantivos incontables como 'interest'.

enunciado:
Choose the correct relative pronoun to complete the sentence:

«That is the house ________ I grew up in.»
justificacion: En este caso se puede usar 'that' o 'which' como pronombre relativo objeto. 'Where' no es correcto porque la oración incluye la preposición 'in'.

enunciado:
Complete the sentence with the correct phrasal verb:

«We need to ________ the new strategy before we present it to the board.»
justificacion: 'Carry out' significa ejecutar o implementar un plan o estrategia. Las otras opciones significan investigar (look into), sobrevivir (get by) y posponer (put off).

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«The (A) informations provided by the (B) company (C) were (D) misleading.»
justificacion: 'Information' es un sustantivo incontable en inglés, por lo que no tiene plural. Debe ser simplemente 'information'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«(A) Despite of the (B) heavy rain, the (C) match (D) continued.»
justificacion: 'Despite' nunca va seguido de 'of'. La forma correcta es 'Despite the rain' o 'In spite of the rain'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«She (A) has visited (B) Paris (C) on last (D) summer.»
justificacion: Al especificar 'last summer' (un tiempo pasado determinado), se debe usar pasado simple ('visited') en lugar de presente perfecto ('has visited').

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«The (A) work was (B) real (C) difficult, but we (D) managed to finish it.»
justificacion: Se necesita un adverbio para modificar al adjetivo 'difficult'. Lo correcto es 'really difficult'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«If (A) I would have (B) known, I (C) would have (D) told you.»
justificacion: En la condición (if-clause) de la tercera condicional no se usa 'would have', sino el pasado perfecto: 'If I had known'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«This car is (A) much more (B) faster than (C) the (D) previous one.»
justificacion: No se puede usar 'more' con un comparativo de adjetivo corto ('faster'). Lo correcto es 'much faster'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«The man (A) whom (B) works here is (C) very (D) kind.»
justificacion: Como 'the man' es el sujeto de la acción 'works', debe usarse el pronombre relativo sujeto 'who', no 'whom'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«I (A) suggest you (B) to talk (C) to the manager (D) immediately.»
justificacion: Tras 'suggest', no se usa infinitivo ('to talk'). Se debe usar la estructura 'suggest that you talk' o 'suggest talking'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«She is (A) married (B) with a (C) famous (D) doctor.»
justificacion: En inglés, el adjetivo 'married' siempre va acompañado de la preposición 'to', no 'with'.

enunciado:
Identify the incorrect part of the sentence:

«There are (A) too many (B) traffic in the (C) city (D) center.»
justificacion: 'Traffic' es un sustantivo incontable. Se debe usar 'too much' en lugar de 'too many'.

enunciado:
Read the text and choose the correct word for each blank:

«Climate change is one of the most critical challenges of our time. Scientists agree that human activity is (1) ______ ____ responsible for the rapid warming of the planet. (2) ______ , international efforts to reduce carbon emissions are often met with political and economic resistance. (3) ________ , many sustainable initiatives are being implemented globally to mitigate the damage.»

justificacion.1: Se necesita un adverbio que modifique al adjetivo 'responsible': 'largely' (en gran medida).
justificacion.2: 'However' (sin embargo) introduce el contraste entre el consenso científico y la resistencia política.
justificacion.3: 'Nevertheless' (no obstante) funciona aquí para indicar que, a pesar de la resistencia, se están tomando medidas.

enunciado:
Read the text and choose the correct word for each blank:

«The history of the internet is fascinating. (1) __________ started as a small military research project in the 1960s,  it has grown into a global network. Today, it is difficult to imagine daily life (2) __________ internet access. (3) ______ , this technological advancement brings new risks, such as privacy concerns and digital security threats.»

justificacion.1: 'What' introduce una cláusula sustantiva ('Lo que comenzó...').
justificacion.2: 'Without' expresa la falta de acceso necesario para la vida diaria.
justificacion.3: 'Moreover' (además) añade otra dimensión (los riesgos) a la descripción del avance tecnológico.

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose where you would see it:

«BREAKFAST FROM 6:00 TO 10:00 A.M.»
justificacion: Un aviso sobre el desayuno (breakfast) se encuentra en un restaurante o tienda de alimentos (food shop).

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose where you would see it:

«You can see the elephants from here. Don't take pictures.»
justificacion: Los elefantes (elephants) se encuentran en un zoológico (zoo).

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose where you would see it:

«Now with lemon.»
justificacion: La frase 'Now with lemon' (ahora con limón) se encuentra en una bebida (drink).

enunciado:
Read the notice and choose where you would see it:

«Wear the right shoes in here.»
justificacion: La advertencia sobre usar el calzado adecuado (wear the right shoes) se encuentra en un gimnasio o sala de deportes (sports room).

enunciado:
